channel.title: Guides on Diego Strebel
channel.description: Recent content in Guides on Diego Strebel
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0

item.title: Running Vivado 2025.2 on NixOS
item.description:
TLDR

I should preface this by saying that Vivado probably has most painful installation process of any program I know. It is as if someone had implemented Wirth’s law. This post is based on Oliver Kovacs great article about his setup for Vivado 2019.2 on NixOS. My changes are based around the new version of Vivadoa and getting the USB connection to an FPGA board to work.

Downloading Vivado

The first step is to download Vivado from the official website. Note that you need to download the offline installer for this to work. To be able to download you will also need to create an AMD account. Then you also need to fill out the export restriction form and handover all your personal information to the US government.

item.title: All the FP64 without CUDA - AMD Radeon Instinct MI50 Review
item.description:
Teardown

Tearing down the card was pretty straightforward. The outer shroud is held on by 6 small screws. When removed, the shroud can be pulled off the card by slightly wiggling it from side to side. Underneath, the card consists of a long black heat sink spanning the whole length of the card. The GPU die is cooled by a copper vapor chamber, easily recognized by its silver fins.
